purpose: A styled scrollbar that appears inside scrollable containers (Panel content, Table body, Popover). Follows the SAP Horizon theme colors and hides/shows based on hover/scroll activity. Rarely rendered directly — it's an artifact of scrollable regions.
whenToUse: Automatically appears in any scrollable container
As a visual indication that content overflows the container
In custom scrollable regions inside cards or dialogs
whenNotToUse: As a decorative element — Scrollbar is always tied to real overflow
Standalone — never rendered outside a scrollable parent
For a progress indicator — use ProgressIndicator instead
For a slider control — use Slider
doRules: Let it appear automatically — don't force-show unless needed for touch
Match SAP Horizon theme (uses sapScrollBar_TrackColor / FaceColor tokens)
Support both vertical and horizontal orientations
Hide when the container is not being interacted with (auto-hide)
dontRules: Do not use as a custom slider — that's not its purpose
Do not hide always — users need to know overflow exists
Do not style with non-theme colors — breaks Horizon consistency
Do not use to indicate progress

layoutGuidance.placement: Right edge (vertical) or bottom edge (horizontal) of scrollable container

examples: Right-edge scrollbar in a scrollable Panel content

responsiveBehavior.S: Always visible on touch (per platform convention)

requirements: Scrollable container is reachable via keyboard (Tab)
Arrow keys scroll when container has focus
Scrollbar thumb has 3:1 contrast against track
No independent aria — the scrollbar is a child of a scrollable container

allowedWith: Any scrollable container (Panel, Table, Popover, Dialog content)
